pdfs.title: INFECTIOUS DISEASE CONSULTATION LETTER
pdfs.facility: Schuylkill Infectious Disease Associates — 833 Spruce St, Philadelphia PA

pages.heading: Re: Margaret Okafor  DOB 03/22/1952  Consult date 04/14/2026
lines: Setting: Inpatient consultation, hospital day 3 of admission for acute
pyelonephritis (admitted 04/12/2026).
REASON FOR CONSULT: Persistent fever on empiric ceftriaxone; urine
culture finalized today with ESBL-producing organism.
SUMMARY: 74F with recurrent UTIs, mechanical mitral valve on warfarin,
T2DM, and CKD stage 3b (eGFR ~34). Urine culture 04/12: E. coli
>100,000 CFU/mL, ESBL PRODUCER.
SUSCEPTIBILITIES:
Ceftriaxone ............ RESISTANT
Ciprofloxacin .......... RESISTANT
Levofloxacin ........... RESISTANT
TMP-SMX ................ RESISTANT (also SJS allergy - NEVER use)
Meropenem .............. SENSITIVE
Ertapenem .............. SENSITIVE
Nitrofurantoin ......... SENSITIVE (avoid: CrCl <40, pyelo)
Fosfomycin ............. SENSITIVE (cystitis only)
COURSE: Transitioned ceftriaxone to meropenem 1 g IV q12h (renal
dosing) with defervescence within 24 hours. Blood cultures no growth.

pages.heading: RECOMMENDATIONS (page 2/2)
lines: 1. Complete 14-day total course: ertapenem 500 mg IV daily (renally
dosed) x10 days via OPAT after discharge.
2. FOR FUTURE FEBRILE UTI EPISODES: recommend EMPIRIC CARBAPENEM
(e.g., meropenem, renally dosed) PENDING CULTURES given documented
ESBL history. Standard empiric ceftriaxone is expected to FAIL
this organism.
3. Avoid TMP-SMX permanently (Stevens-Johnson syndrome).
4. Fluoroquinolones: resistant, and QTc 470 ms is a further caution.
5. Fever with a mechanical mitral prosthesis: maintain a low
threshold to draw blood cultures BEFORE antibiotics and consider
TEE if bacteremia or persistent fever (endocarditis evaluation).
6. Urology referral for recurrent UTI workup (placed).
Thank you for this consultation. Happy to re-consult on any
recurrence.
Ngozi Adeyemi, MD — Infectious Disease
Schuylkill Infectious Disease Associates

pdfs.title: WARFARIN MANAGEMENT FLOWSHEET
pdfs.facility: Liberty Anticoagulation Clinic — 4200 Frankford Ave, Philadelphia PA

pages.heading: Patient: Okafor, Margaret  DOB 03/22/1952
lines: INDICATION: Mechanical mitral valve replacement (St. Jude bileaflet,
09/2015). TARGET INR RANGE: 2.5 - 3.5. Duration: LIFELONG.
NOTE: Mechanical MITRAL prosthesis - do NOT hold or reverse warfarin
without cardiology involvement; if interruption required, bridging
with heparin per cardiology.
CURRENT REGIMEN: Warfarin 5 mg PO daily; 2.5 mg on Sundays
(32.5 mg/week).
INR HISTORY:
07/10/2026   INR 2.7   In range      No dose change
06/19/2026   INR 3.1   In range      No dose change
05/29/2026   INR 2.4   Slightly low  Sunday dose 2.5 -> full 5 mg
for 1 week, then resume; recheck 3 wks
04/16/2026   INR 2.9   In range      Hospital discharge check
04/12/2026   INR 2.6   In range      On admission
03/06/2026   INR 2.8   In range      No dose change
INTERACTIONS FLAGGED: Antibiotics frequently potentiate warfarin
(esp. TMP-SMX - also ALLERGY/SJS, fluoroquinolones, metronidazole).
Recheck INR within 3-5 days of starting any antibiotic.
Next scheduled INR: 08/07/2026.
Managing pharmacist: R. Delgado, PharmD, CACP
